hover_line: A bare room. Light on. Nothing moving.
vignette_text:
Just a room.
Table, chair, laptop.
Yesterday someone paced here, talking too loud on the phone.
Today it’s resting.
Guess even empty rooms get days off.
outcome_a_text:
I give it a second.
Still empty.
Fair enough.
outcome_b_text:
Nothing’s happening here.
Can’t blame the room.
Onward.
reflection_a_text:
The light doesn’t change.
Nothing to catch, nothing to miss.
Just a room being a room.
reflection_b_text:
I’m not here to admire furniture.
There are better windows than this.
At least there used to be.
post_hover_line: Still empty. The light hasn’t moved.

hover_line: Blue light flickers behind half-drawn curtains.
vignette_text:
A TV’s on somewhere inside.
Laugh track, canned and late.
Someone’s eating straight from the pan.
I can’t see them, but I know the rhythm.
Evenings sound the same when you stop listening.
outcome_a_text:
I let it play out.
Same timing, same voices.
Some things don’t surprise me anymore.
outcome_b_text:
I stop listening halfway through.
I get the idea.
That’s usually enough.
reflection_b_text:
The laughter leaks out anyway.
Same timing, different room.
It’s just something to fill air.
memory_fragment_text:
Dinners were quiet after the shouting stopped.
The TV filled the space better than we could.
Nobody asked questions when something else was talking.
post_hover_line: The TV’s still on. Someone changed the channel.

hover_line: A child leans over the sill, someone older nearby.
vignette_text:
The kid’s asking questions.
Too many, too fast.An older voice answers without hurrying.
No shouting, no edge to it.
Just time being spent.
outcome_a_text:
I stay longer than planned.
Doesn’t feel intrusive.
Just quiet.
outcome_b_text:
That’s their evening.
No need to hover.
They’re doing fine.
reflection_b_text:
It’s strange how calm it sounds.
Like nothing is about to break.
I forget people can have that.
memory_fragment_text:
Someone used to answer me like that.
Slow, patient, never annoyed.
I don’t remember their face clearly.
Just how safe it felt to ask.
post_hover_line: The window’s still open. The voices moved away.

hover_line: Curtains drawn tight. No light leaks through.
vignette_text:
Can’t see anything inside.
Just fabric and a bit of movement.
No sound, either.
Someone’s home, probably.
Or maybe not.
outcome_a_text:
My brain starts anyway.
It always does.
Even without clues.
outcome_b_text:
Not everything needs a story.
This can stay closed.
reflection_b_text:
It’s quieter when I don’t guess.
No versions, no endings.
Just a window I don’t know.
memory_fragment_text:
I used to imagine what was happening in other rooms.
It felt safer than checking.
Sometimes I was right.
That never made it better.
post_hover_line: The curtains don’t move. Still nothing to see.

hover_line: Two figures move around the same small kitchen.
vignette_text:
They’re cooking together.
Not talking much.
Someone reaches for the same drawer twice.
An apology, automatic.
They keep going.
outcome_a_text:
This feels familiar enough.
No sparks, no disasters.
Just effort.
outcome_b_text:
Looks stable from here.
That’s usually enough.
reflection_b_text:
It’s quiet without being tense.
Everyone knows their role.
Nothing spills over.
memory_fragment_text:
I was in something like this once.
Not unhappy, just careful.
I didn’t give more than I had.
No one asked me to stay.
post_hover_line: The light’s still on. They’re eating now.

vignette_text:
Lights go out one by one.
A few stay on, doing nothing in particular.
Traffic thins. Voices don’t carry as far.
The building looks flatter without people in it.
Like it’s done for the night.
outcome_a_text:
Might as well enjoy the quiet.
It doesn’t last long.
outcome_b_text:
That’s enough for today.
No need to drag it out.
reflection_a_text:
Nothing else is going to happen tonight.
The city’s already halfway asleep.
I’m not missing much.
reflection_b_text:
The day doesn’t need a closing scene.
It can just stop here.
That works too.
post_hover_line: Just dark windows. The night’s settled.

hover_line: A light clicks on. Someone moves closer to the glass.
vignette_text:
I’m not the only one looking.
They stop for a second, adjusting their eyes.
We notice each other at about the same time.
No rush. No anger.
Just an unexpected overlap.
outcome_a_text:
Well.
Guess that’s me, then.
Standing here.
outcome_b_text:
Right.
Too much reality for one balcony.
Done.
reflection_a_text:
They’ll forget me in ten seconds.
I’ll forget their face in five.
That’s fair.
memory_fragment_text:
I learned early not to stay too long.
Leaving quietly kept things simple.
Most of the time, no one asked where I went.
And I didn’t volunteer it.
post_hover_line: The window’s dark again. Nothing follows.

hover_line: Raised voices slip through an open window.
vignette_text:
Two people, same room.
Same volume, different words.
One of them laughs at the wrong time.
The other doesn’t like that.
It keeps circling back.
outcome_a_text:
I let it run for a bit.
Same loop, same energy.
Predictable.
outcome_b_text:
I cut it off mid-sentence.
Not my show.
Not tonight.
reflection_a_text:
They’ll keep going with or without me.
People always do.
I don’t need the whole track.
memory_fragment_text:
I used to do this with real shouting.
Turn it into background, pretend it’s normal.
It worked in the moment.
It still shows up anyway.
post_hover_line: The window’s still open. They lowered their voices.
